name: Law on Personal Data Protection (Law No. 91/2025/QH15)
summary: Vietnam's comprehensive personal data protection law, passed June 2025 and effective 1 January 2026, replacing the earlier Decree 13/2023. It governs personal data processing activities by domestic and foreign entities handling Vietnamese citizens' data, with data localization requirements and revenue-based penalties.
keyRequirements: Obtain consent for personal data processing with specific form and content requirements
Mandatory data breach notification within 72 hours to the Department of Cybersecurity
Conduct impact assessments for cross-border data transfers and file with authorities
Appoint a data protection department or officer for organizations processing large volumes of data
Data subject rights including access, correction, deletion, and withdrawal of consent
Data localization requirements with copies of data stored in Vietnam for cross-border transfers
Register and declare data processing activities with the Ministry of Public Security
penalties: For sale or purchase of personal data: fines of 10x revenue from the sale or VND 3 billion (~USD 120,000), whichever is higher. For cross-border transfer violations: 5% of prior year's revenue or VND 3 billion. Enforced by the Department of Cybersecurity and High-Tech Crime Prevention under the Ministry of Public Security.
